title: Cleanup, Partial Initialization, and Error Paths · Mastery Check
summary: A safety board presents unfamiliar cleanup, partial initialization, and error paths decisions across code, diagrams, diagnostics, and stakeholder evidence; each distractor corresponds to a named misconception. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for failure-atomic resource constructor. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.
Release acquired resources in dependency-safe reverse order with idempotent or guarded cleanup.
Use structured goto cleanup when it centralizes unwinding without skipping initialization or obscuring ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Partial Initialization State · changed case · Quiz Partial Initialization State
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz native-systems team handles memory case 4856. Produce bounded C evidence to unwind partially acquired resources in reverse order; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Represent which subresources are initialized so cleanup is valid after every acquisition point.

Which action demonstrates Partial Initialization State without dropping earlier contracts?
